old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.66","title":"Define transactional OSCORE context persistence","description":"Custom/persistent OscoreContextStore implementations need an atomic put contract and durable sequence reservation. new_value: {"notes":"Implemented transactional OSCORE persistence with backward-compatible in-memory OscoreContextStore, structural TransactionalOscoreContextStore contract, durable SQLite backend, atomic peer/context generation CAS, permanent canonical COSE sender nonce ledgers, pre-use sequence block reservation, crash/fork-safe reopen, recipient replay CAS persisted before plaintext delivery, pin-preserving tombstones, store-backed TOFU migration, cancellation-definitive transactions, owner-only 0600 SQLite files and secure parent validation, opaque endpoint preservation, and async EDHOC responder publication. Final independent security/API reviews found no in-scope P0-P2 issues; correlation lifecycle finding remains tracked by 2auf.65 and was not expanded under current-work-only directive. Validation: uv frozen CoAP+crypto 265 passed; Ruff lint passed; git diff check passed; adjusted strict mypy passed in implementation review. Ruff format check flags pre-existing whole-file formatting in resources.py only; no broad unrelated reformat performed."}
Transactional context publication and durable nonce/replay persistence implemented, reviewed, and validated.
